title: Limonium peregrinum
taxonomy: Limonium peregrinum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. It belongs to the genus Limonium, which includes various other species of coastal plants. The plant is also known as sea lavender, due to its purple flowers that resemble those of the lavender plant. The classification of Limonium peregrinum is further supported by its physical characteristics, such as its succulent leaves and stems. As a member of the Plumbaginaceae family, Limonium peregrinum is closely related to other plants with similar adaptations to coastal environments.
uses: Limonium peregrinum has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Its leaves and flowers are rich in essential oils that have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. In some regions, it is used to treat wounds, burns, and skin irritations due to its antiseptic properties. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal applications and potential benefits.

properties: Limonium peregrinum has been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential therapeutic uses. intro: Limonium peregrinum, also known as sea lavender, is a perennial plant that thrives in salt-tolerant environments and is widely distributed along coastal areas of Europe, North Africa, and the Mediterranean region. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Limonium peregrinum include limonoids, flavonoids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Limonium peregrinum is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications such as creams and salves to soothe and calm skin irritations.
